description_long: This object is a large solar energy infrastructure installation featuring multiple rectangular solar panels mounted on a broad, flat base. The panels are arranged in a staggered, multi-level configuration, with some panels tilted at various angles to optimize sunlight capture. The base appears to be constructed from a combination of metal and reinforced materials, providing stability and support for the elevated panels. Several structural supports and mounting brackets are visible, suggesting a robust and engineered design. The color palette is dominated by dark grays and blues, with the solar panels exhibiting a grid-like pattern typical of photovoltaic cells. The overall structure is expansive, likely intended for installation in open urban spaces such as rooftops, parking lots, or dedicated solar farms.
description: A large, multi-panel solar energy installation with staggered, tilted panels on a sturdy, metallic base. The structure features grid-patterned photovoltaic surfaces and robust support elements.
description_view_0: From this view, the staggered arrangement and varying tilt angles of the solar panels are prominent, with the base and support structures clearly visible.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the length of the installation and the angular orientation of the panels, showing their elevation above the base.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals the depth of the structure and the alignment of the panels, with the support framework more visible.
description_view_3: This opposite side view emphasizes the spread and distribution of the panels, as well as the mounting supports and base extensions.
attribute_panel_orientation: Panels are tilted at approximately 25-35 degrees, with varying azimuth directions; orientation appears fixed.
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with visible metal (likely aluminum or steel) support frames and brackets.
attribute_array_configuration: Multiple rows of panels arranged in a staggered, clustered configuration; approximately 6-8 panels in total.
